schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The generic driver-executor — the fifth spine step (MAP.md:888), and the first device that COMPOSES the ones before it. Given a declarative driver `{name, method, why, target:{table, owner}}`, `GroundLoopDevice.run_driver` (1) resolves the method-pointer against the method-registry and REFUSES if it is not in proven-space (Law 8); (2) runs the proven method to produce a row; (3) writes that row to the driver's OWNED target through db_domain, whose owner-gate refuses any non-owner write (Law 6); (4) returns a RUN-RECORD, so the run is legible — LEARNING, not silent RUNNING. `MethodRegistry` (registry.py) is proven-space made resolvable: a method is admitted only if its proof passes UNDER THE TESTER (Law 8 as physics, not a label). `GroundLoopDevice` subclasses `BaseDevice` (carries CP1-CP6, reports the Form v0 #2 surface); its one capability is `run_driver`.
why: Most of what a device DOES — collect a metric, watch a threshold, alarm, fold artifacts into the trees — is not bespoke per-device code but the SAME loop with different (trigger, method, target). If that holds, ONE generic executor runs all of them from declarative data, and there is no uninstrumented daemon left to rot silently (the UU disease: dozens of components failing silently for months, producing success-shaped records). This device is the operational-driver primitive's executor (operational-driver.json; MAP 'the operational-driver primitive'). It introduces NO third authority — proven-space is the tester's, the owned target is db_domain's; the ground_loop only wires them under a driver's declared why. That composition IS the point: the spine's parts, first working as a system.
invoke: import: `from cairn.ground_loop.loop import GroundLoopDevice; from cairn.ground_loop.registry import MethodRegistry`. Wire a method into proven-space (`registry.register(name, method, proof_path)` — admitted only if the proof passes under the tester), build a driver dict, then `GroundLoopDevice(registry=reg).run_driver(driver)`. Prove: `python3 cairn/ground_loop/proofs/test_ground_loop.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: device — the generic executor the operational-driver node-class is run BY (operational-driver.json's 'candidate: the ground_loop is that executor' — this stone confirms it for the simple case).
entry: FRESH design to Form v0. No mechanism grafted — the ground_loop is Cairn's own synthesis of three ratified designs: the operational-driver primitive (behavior as declarative data), the state-vocabulary's LEARNING-not-RUNNING rule (every run emits evidence), and the two guards drawn straight from the laws (Law 8 proven-space, Law 6 owner-gate). It reuses the tester and db_domain as COMPONENTS, not as quarry.
state: PROVED for the minimal stone — the HYPOTHESIS (ground_loop = the generic driver-executor) CONFIRMED for the simple sensor case: one `run_driver` wires an arbitrary proven method to an arbitrary owned target; metrics / alarms / the harvest differ only in (method, target, trigger), the fields the driver carries. Teeth, all green (and dogfooded under the tester; a falsification pass confirms the Law 8 guard bites — bypassing `resolve` reds the proof): the happy path lands the method's row in the owned target (read back through db_domain) and returns a run-record; an unproven method-pointer is refused AND a method whose proof reds is refused admission (Law 8); a non-owner target write is refused by db_domain's gate through the ground_loop (Law 6); a malformed driver is refused loudly before anything runs (CP1); it is a device (Law 2 / Form v0 #2). OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) it does not SCHEDULE — `run_driver` runs one FIRED pass; triggers (interval/date/quantity/a-state), reactive/scheduled waking, queue draining, and back-edge (kick-back) routing belong to the host/rack device (the next spine step) and the emit-chokepoint; (b) run-records are PRODUCED, not yet journaled/persisted — a durable journey is a later stone (the rack consumes them); the target WRITE is durable, the run-record is the legibility surface (mirrors the tester's produced-not-persisted discipline); (c) the method-registry is IN-MEMORY — a durable, restart-surviving registry waits on a consumer that needs it; (d) v0 methods take no arguments and return a row — a driver's `sources` (inputs to a transform, not just a sensor read) firm up when a transform-driver pulls them.
gates: proof gate (tester) — proofs/test_ground_loop.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester; needs Postgres (db_domain's one-time role). Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) `run_driver` runs a method that did not resolve to proven-space, or the registry admits a method whose proof did not pass under the tester (Law 8 breached — a driver introduced unproven code); (2) a target write that is not the declared owner's is NOT refused (Law 6 breached — an ambient write); (3) a driver runs but leaves no run-record, or a refused run returns a success-shaped result instead of raising loudly (Law 7 — a silent or success-masked failure, the UU disease); (4) the happy path does not land the method's row in its target; (5) `GroundLoopDevice` stops subclassing `BaseDevice` / drops CP1-CP6, or `introspect()` stops reporting intention -> state -> settings in order. Wrong-shape signal (refutes the hypothesis, not just this build): a real driver arises whose behavior CANNOT be expressed as (proven-method → owned-target) run by this one executor — then the primitive is too narrow, kicked back to design (CP2), not forced.
how_it_learns: Yes — it is the executor that makes 'everything running is LEARNING' true rather than aspirational. Every `run_driver` emits a run-record (what ran, why, what it wrote, when), so there is no silent execution: a driver that stops producing what its why claims shows up as a red run-record / a refused write, kicked back to design at the point of creation (CP2 — FAIL = Further Advance In Learning), never a success-shaped silence. The drivers it runs ARE the system's standing learners (the graph-tree harvester and the 'sleep' consolidation are themselves operational-drivers). Its own horizon: the hypothesis is confirmed only for the simple sensor case — whether ONE executor also covers scheduled/batched drivers, transforms with sources, and back-edge routing is measured as the rack and more drivers pull those; if one breaks the shape, the primitive is revised, not the work routed around it.
traces_to: Law 8 (a driver wires only proven methods — proven-space is the tester's, resolved through the method-registry)
Law 6 (writes route through the target owner's gate — db_domain's, never ambient)
Law 3 (the hypothesis is stated so it can be refuted; run-records are measured evidence)
Law 7 (a refused run is loud, not success-shaped — no silent failure)
Law 4 (the guards are physics propagated from the composed devices, not this executor's policy)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address)
Law 1 (behavior-as-data run once, not re-derived per device)
operational-driver node-class (the ground_loop is its executor)
state-machine-physics child: 'ground_loop-as-driver-executor — confirm or refute the hypothesis'
build order: db domain -> ground loop -> rack (MAP.md:888)
owner: CC owns the ground_loop code + its proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the operational-driver node-class definition and the state vocabulary the drivers rest in (LEARNING). Proven-space is owned by the tester; each target is owned by its own owner — the ground_loop owns neither, it only wires them.
proof: proofs/test_ground_loop.py — the hypothesis end-to-end (proven method -> owned target; row lands, run-record emitted); unproven code cannot run (unregistered refused + red-proof admission refused, Law 8); a non-owner target write is refused (Law 6); a malformed driver is refused loudly (CP1); device-hood + the six + Form v0 #2 order. Composes the tester (proven-space) and db_domain (owned target); dogfooded under the tester (green VALIDATION); a falsification pass confirms the Law 8 guard bites. fixtures/collect.py + fixtures/proof_collect.py — a proven sensor method and the proof that admits it. Green: `python3 cairn/ground_loop/proofs/test_ground_loop.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 as the fifth step of the code spine (base -> base pair -> tester -> db_domain -> ground_loop), immediately after the sudo_relay 'thing-for-akien-along-the-way'. The first device to COMPOSE the spine — it wires the tester's proven-space to db_domain's owned target under a driver's why. Confirms operational-driver.json's candidate ('the ground_loop is that executor') for the simple case. NEXT on the spine (MAP.md:888): the rack (host device) — the scheduler/sensors that FIRE drivers (triggers, reactive/scheduled waking, queue draining), then inference domain -> web server. Deferred until pulled: durable method-registry, journaled run-records, transform-drivers with sources, back-edge routing.
